Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Continued To Nov-27-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Hearing (Motions) (Motions)
Off Calendar Oct-11-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:10 am, pausing at 9:37 am, resuming at 9:56 am and concluding at 10:07 am.

Plaintiff Julie Lee is present, represented by Sydney J Hall, Esq. is placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Yi Ya Chang not appearing.

Plaintiff's counsel presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

JULIE LEE is examined by the Court.

COURT FINDS:

1. The 4th Amended complaint filed Jul-14-2022 is the operative pleading.
2. The DOES in this matter were dismissed on Jun-17-2024
3. Default against Defendant Yi Ya Chang was entered on Sept-12-2023.
4. The Plaintiff has made a prima facia as to the alligations
5. The requested judgment is supported by the evidence and will resolve the entire action.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Julie Lee, and against Defendant Yi Ya Chang, as follows:

1. Damages: $49,890.53
2. Prejudgment Interest: $17,624.34
3. Costs: $1,726.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $69,240.87

Proposed judgment signed and filed. (D505)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HONG CHEW'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. The meet and confer declaration does not explain how the parties met and conferred. (See CCP 430.41(a)(3)(A) [declaration shall state "means" of meeting and conferring]; Granahan Decl., par. 3.) In addition, plaintiff has indicated a desire to file a Fourth Amended Complaint in response to this demurrer. The parties are ordered to meet and confer and see if they can stipulate to the filing of an amended complaint. The response to the Third Amended Complaint is now due by April 29, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: Defendant's counsel sent an email to the court requesting a hearing on this matter. There being no appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANT HONG CHEW'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar. Plaintiff filed an untimely response indicating she intends to amend her first amended complaint. Plaintiff is admonished that an amended pleading must be filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer. (Code Civ. Proc. 472(a).) Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to file a second amended complaint.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, email:tonyvaughn@yahoo.com
